{"society_power": 7.0, "state_power": 9.0, "society_change": 0.0, "state_change": 0.5, "key_event": "WWII mobilization and permanent national-security state", "reasoning": "War and Cold War drove dramatic, durable state expansion (mass taxation, standing military, CIA/DoD, loyalty programs, internment), pushing state power toward the overwhelming range; society mobilized strongly (labor peak, Great Migration, GI Bill, incipient civil rights) but offsetting setbacks (Taft-Hartley, internment, loyalty screening) left net society power roughly unchanged."}
